title: Clean and Safe Team Homeless Actions and Outcomes - February 2026
resolved_text:
1
Memorandum
DATE: April 6, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
DEPT: Community Engagement and Services
SUBJECT: Clean and Safe Team Homeless Actions and Outcomes - February 2026

The City of Reno continues to address homeless-related concerns and provide outreach to the unsheltered community within city limits through the Clean and Safe Team. The Clean and Safe Team interdepartmental team plays a critical role in addressing the multifaceted challenges and community impacts associated with homelessness, while coordinating multiple concurrent efforts and programs to ensure a comprehensive, citywide response.

Clean and Safe Team - Departments
• Licensing and Code Enforcement
• Community Engagement and Services – Clean and Safe, Reno Direct
• City Manager's Office - Security
• Housing and Neighborhood Development
• Maintenance and Operations
• Parks and Recreation
• Reno Police – Community Action and Outreach (CAO), Mobile Engagement Team (MET), Parking Enforcement

The following is an overview of February 2026 data, actions, outcomes, and updates from the Clean and Safe Team:

February 2026 Key Highlights
• 8 community engagement and outreach efforts
• 55 outreach interactions
• 11 housing arrangements
• 91 citations
• 80 arrests
2

• 2 large encampment clean-ups
• 21 smaller encampment clean-ups
• 127 yards of trash collected
• 42 occupied vehicles posted
• 10 occupied vehicles towed
• 1 park exclusion
• 43 Code Enforcement cases
• 104 Allied Security site visits

February Updates
• Reno Housing Authority Motel Assistance Program: In February, Clean and Safe Outreach facilitated 7 individuals into the Motel Assistance program. Four individuals successfully transitioned out of the Motel Assistance Program into permanent housing.
• Camera Mobile Trailers: Clean and Safe's Mobile Camera Trailer Pilot Program is ongoing. The mobile trailers continue to keep previously chronic areas clear and provide an extra tool for Clean and Safe staff to monitor. The mobile trailers are currently installed at Spokane Street, along the railroad and Galletti Way, along the railroad.

Community Engagement and Partnerships

Multiple departments partake in standing or topic specific meetings with regional partners to help address homelessness, identify support, and advance collaborative, solution-oriented initiatives. February highlights are as follows:

February Meeting Department Purpose and Outcomes
February 4: Moana, Kietzke, Gentry Business Center Meeting Community Engagement and Services, Reno Police Department Topics discussed at this meeting included Fusus Program, and reporting process for encampments and trespassing.
February 4: Washoe County Outreach Case Conferencing Community Engagement and Services, Housing and Neighborhood Development One individual was referred to Safe Camp and the individual was approved by the outreach teams. The individual moved in on February 12.
February 10: Downtown Reno Partnership Security and Safety Collaboration Code Enforcement, Community Engagement and Services, Reno Police Department Clean and Safe, Reno Police Department, and Code Enforcement meet with residents, casino staff, and other jurisdictions to discuss issues, upcoming events, and ongoing activities in the downtown corridor.
February 12: Regional Alliance to end Homelessness (RAH) Community Engagement and Services, and Housing and Neighborhood Development A monthly meeting of stakeholders who work to end homelessness across the region to discuss issues and facilitate cross agency collaboration.
February 18: Washoe County Outreach Case Conferencing Community Engagement and Services, Housing and Neighborhood Development One individual was referred to Safe Camp and the individual was approved by the outreach teams. The individual moved in on February 25.
3

February 23: City of Reno and Nevada Department of Transportation ILA Meeting Community Engagement and Services Established a response process for the City of Reno and Nevada Department of Transportation (NDOT) Interlocal Agreement to address encampments on NDOT Right-of-Ways.
February 26: School of Public Health Street Health Collaborative Meeting Community Engagement and Services A monthly meeting with street outreach workers and community providers who convene to discuss homeless-related concerns, review programs, and share community resources.
February 27: City of Reno and Cares Campus Collaboration Community Engagement and Services, Housing and Neighborhood Development Review shelter clients' housing plans for shelter clients with income and streamline efforts to transition shelter clients into permanent housing. Meetings launched in the Fall 2025.

Outreach and Interactions

Clean and Safe
Interactions Housing Arrangements Shelter Arrangements Identification Replacements Family Reunification Resources Accessed
55 11 2 0 0 36


Outreach Interactions
Location Individual Interactions Service Acceptance Outcomes
City Hall 1 1
Client arrived at City Hall to inquire about the Permanent Supportive Housing waitlist status, no updates at this time.

Client is currently staying at El Cortez and asked about the next steps for housing. Discussed Safe Camp as an interim option and the client wants to proceed. The client will be brought forward for approval at the next Safe Camp meeting.
Oddie Blvd & Silverada Blvd 0 0 Arrived on site for the City Walk to assist with trash and debris removal.
6th Street & Elko Avenue 1 1 Met with client to fill out Safe Camp paperwork. Client moved in on February 2.
Siegel Suites - Victorian 1 1 Extended client's motel stay, as the new projected move-in date to Silverada Manor is on February 18.
Washoe County: Case Conferencing 0 1 Brought a client forward for approval at the Safe Camp meeting. The client was approved by the case conferencing group and the client moved into Safe Camp on February 12.
Teams Meeting 2 2 Met with Washoe County Guardian's Office to discuss two clients who have reached the limit of Reno Housing Authority's Motel Assistance Program and discussed next steps. The Washoe County Guardian's Office agreed to take over motel payments until the two clients move-in to the two clients' permanent housing unit.
Siegel Suites - Nevadan 1 1 Informed client the client has been approved for a move-in on February 5 at the Village on Sage Street through the City of Reno's Rapid Rehousing Program.
Village on Sage Street 1 1 Facilitated a successful move-in to the Village on Sage Street.
Siegel Suites – Nevadan 1 1 Extended client's stay at the Siegel Suites Nevadan, while the client awaits move-in to the client's permanent housing unit.
Phone Call 1 1 Connected with the client by phone while the client was in the hospital to confirm the client's interest in moving to Safe Camp. The client confirmed and move-in scheduled for February 12.


Highland Avenue & Evans Avenue 1 0 Scheduled clean-up. Individual declined services.
Railroad, from Record Street to Park Street 2 0 Scheduled clean-up. Spoke with two individuals about Cares Campus. The two individuals declined a ride and stated the two individuals would get to Cares Campus on the two individuals' own.
Ramada Inn - Diamond's 0 0 Received updated receipts from Ramada Inn and submitted updated receipts to Reno Housing Authority.
Sage St/Railroad 4 0 Scheduled clean-up. Spoke with four individuals; the four individuals declined services.
Siegel Suites - Nevadan 1 1 Extended motel stay for client while the client awaits approval for Lucky Motel through HUD-VASH voucher.
Lucky Motel 1 1 Facilitated move-in for client at the Lucky Motel through the HUD-VASH voucher.
Wells Avenue & Kuenzli Street 4 2 Spoke with 4 individuals in the area. One individual requested a Verification of Homelessness letter to begin the one individual's housing process. Clean and Safe will write the Verification of Homelessness letter and deliver the Verification of Homelessness letter to the one individual.

One individual requested a new ID, so the one individual can start the one individual's housing process. Scheduled a DMV appointment through Cares Campus on February 17.
Sage St/Railroad 1 0 Spoke with one individual camping on the side of the road. The one individual declined services and agreed the one individual would vacate the area.
Safe Camp 1 1 Facilitated a successful move-in to Safe Camp.
Siegel Suites - Nevadan 1 1 Assisted AG with moving into the Siegel Suites while AG awaits placement at the Village on Sage through the City of Reno's Rapid Rehousing Program.
Teams Meeting 0 0 Sat in on client's Competency Court hearing to determine whether the client could move in with the client's mom. It was determined the client and the client's mom cannot live together long-term. The plan is to find a new housing plan for the client's mom.
CARES Campus 1 1 Returned personal property to client that was stored after the client was arrested.
EconoLodge 3 3 Facilitated a successful move to Econo Lodge through the Reno Housing Authority's Motel
Assistance program for two adults and one minor child.
Washoe County: Case Conferencing 0 1 Brought a client forward for approval at the Safe Camp meeting. The client was approved by the outreach teams and the client moved into Safe Camp on February 25.
Siegel Suites - Victorian 1 1 Extended motel stay for client as the client's move-in to Silverada Manor had been pushed back again.
Phone Call 0 0 Spoke with the Washoe County Guardian's Office about a mom and son to review the mom and son's case plan. The Washoe County Guardian's Office will now cover the mom and son's motel costs since the mom and son are no longer eligible for Reno Housing Authority's Motel Assistance program.
EconoLodge 2 2 Facilitated a move-in at the Econo Lodge through Reno Housing Authority's Motel Assistance Program for a couple after the couple was evicted from the Cheryl Apartments. Plan is to identify and submit applications for alternative housing options.
Siegel Suites - Nevadan 1 1 Facilitated a move-in for a client at the Village on Sage Street through the Rapid Rehousing Program.
Siegel Suites - Victorian 1 1 Extended a client's motel stay through February 26 at the Siegel Suites and confirmed the client's move-in to Silverada Manor apartments that same day.
Lemmon Drive 2 0 Arrived on-site per a Service Request for a young woman in the area. Unable to locate the individual described but engaged with one male who declined services and one woman who was selling flowers to earn rent money. Referred the woman to Housing and Neighborhood Development for rental assistance.
Brodhead Park 1 1 Followed up with a client who had previously requested a Verification of Homelessness letter for housing. The Verification of Homelessness letter was provided to the client and submitted to Reno Housing Authority.
Sutro Street & Commercial Row 2 0 Scheduled clean-up. Engaged with a couple - one adult male and one adult female, who was 8 months pregnant.

The couple was interested in reuniting with family in Sacramento, California. Karma Box was deployed to purchase a bus ticket, however the couple declined services, stating the couple was not ready to proceed at that time.
CARES Campus 1 1 Met with a client to fill out paperwork for the Motel Assistance program and discuss housing options for the future. Plan is to submit paperwork and get the client moved into a motel within the next couple of days.
La Rue Avenue & Virginia Street 1 0 An encampment was located on both public and private property. Outreach was provided and the individual declined services.
Wedekind Road 2 1 Scheduled clean-up. Engaged with two individuals: one individual who declined services and the other individual explained the other individual was working with Washoe County HOPE team but lost contact. Contacted the Washoe County HOPE team and got the other individual connected with the Washoe County HOPE team. The Washoe County HOPE team scheduled time to meet the other individual and further discuss housing options.
6th Street & Eureka Avenue 1 0 Outreach provided and the individual declined services.
7th Street & Sutro Street 1 0 Outreach provided and the individual declined services.
Sutro Street & Commercial Row 1 0 Arrived on site to reconnect with the couple from the previous day. Spoke with the male, who reported the male had taken the pregnant woman to the hospital the night before. Revisited reunification options and other available services, however the male reiterated that the couple is not ready to access services.
4th Street & Ferrari Street 3 1 Arrived on site for the City Walk and engaged with 3 individuals. A couple explained the couple was on the Reno Housing Authority waitlist and awaiting a follow-up. After contacting Reno Housing Authority, educated the couple on the steps to maintain the couple's spot on the Reno Housing Authority waitlist, and the benefits of working with a case manager at CARES to obtain the couple's essential documents and support communication with Reno Housing Authority. The couple declined services.

The third individual was on a 30-day sit out from CARES and expressed interest in substance-use treatment to reconnect with family. Coordinated with Karma Box to transport the third individual to Mill Street Care Center.
CARES Campus 1 1 Returned personal belongings that were stored after an arrest.
EconoLodge 1 1 Connected with a client to discuss the Reno Housing Authority waitlist and the Rapid Rehousing Program, and assisted the client with signing up for the Reno Housing Authority waitlist for subsidized housing.
Siegel Suites - Nevadan 1 1 Checked client into the Siegel Suites for the Motel Assistance program while awaiting to hear back on applications at the Vintage at Washington and the Point Apartments.
Siegel Suites - Victorian 2 2 Successfully facilitated a move in for client and the client's new live-in caregiver at Silverada Manor.
Virginia Lake Park 1 0 Arrived on site and observed an individual in the playground who appeared semi-conscious. Provided outreach, the individual declined services. Encouraged the individual to leave the playground and the individual vacated.
CARES Campus 0 1 Arrived on site to pick-up a client's belongings to store as the client is currently hospitalized and property cannot be stored past 7 days in Safe Camp storage.
Gentry Way & Kietzke Lane 0 0 Arrived on site to search for a family with a child that was reportedly seen at Gentry Way & Kietzke Lane. Unable to locate the family.
Total 55 36

Enforcement and Compliance
Reno Police Department
Reno Police Department's Community Action and Outreach (CAO) and Mobile Engagement Team (MET) outcomes:
• Unsheltered Contacts: 15
• Posted Occupied Vehicles: 42
• Vehicles Towed: 10
• Citations: 84
• Felony Arrests: 17
• Misdemeanor Arrests: 63

Park and River Rangers
In February, the Park and River Rangers conducted 761 site-visits. The Park and River Rangers made 71 interactions with unsheltered individuals, which included issuing warnings, citations, or providing service referrals.

Park and River Rangers enforcement efforts in City Parks and along the Truckee River:
• Citations issued: 7 o 7 citations issued for sitting, lying, sleeping, or camping on a public place
• Park exclusions issued: 1 o 1 Mira Loma Park

Code Enforcement
Code Enforcement cases related to homeless related concerns on private property:
• Code Enforcement Cases: 43
• Courtesy Letters: 15
• Warning of Violation: 8
• First Administrative Citation: 3
• Second Administrative Citation: 2
• Third Administrative Citation: 1

Allied Universal Security
There are two Allied Universal Security officers dedicated to Clean and Safe, patrolling chronic hotspots, monitoring recent cleanup efforts, and issuing written notices to encampments in public spaces. Notices support expediating enforcement efforts.

Large Clean-Up Operations
Large clean-up operations include 3 or more established encampments and/or large amounts of debris, which require the Clean and Safe provider's services.

Sage Street & Railroad and Highland Avenue & Evans Avenue
• Number of Notices Issued: 21
• Number of Yards of Trash Collected: 20

Sutro Street & Railroad, Sutro Street & Wedekind Road, Highland Avenue & Evans Avenue, and 7th Street & Wells Avenue
• Number of Notices Issued: 6
• Number of Yards of Trash Collected: 17

Union Pacific Clean-Up
The City of Reno has monthly coordinated clean-up efforts with Union Pacific. Due to inclement weather in February, Union Pacific was unable to travel over the mountain pass, resulting in the cancellation of the scheduled clean-up.

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
fields.tldr: The Clean and Safe Team's February 2026 report details interdepartmental efforts to address homelessness in Reno, including 55 outreach interactions resulting in 11 housing arrangements and 2 shelter arrangements. Enforcement activities included 91 citations, 80 arrests, 23 encampment clean-ups, and 127 yards of trash collected. Reno Direct received 323 service requests related to homeless concerns, occupied vehicles, trespassing, and camping, a 1% decrease from January. Key program updates include 7 individuals facilitated into the Reno Housing Authority Motel Assistance Program and ongoing deployment of Mobile Camera Trailers at chronic hotspot locations.
fields.key_stats: 55 outreach interactions; 11 housing arrangements; 2 shelter arrangements; 91 citations; 80 arrests (17 felony, 63 misdemeanor); 2 large and 21 smaller encampment clean-ups; 127 yards of trash collected; 42 occupied vehicles posted; 10 vehicles towed; 1 park exclusion; 43 Code Enforcement cases; 104 Allied Universal Security site visits; 761 Park and River Ranger site visits; 71 Ranger interactions with unsheltered individuals; 323 Reno Direct service requests; 7 individuals facilitated into Motel Assistance Program; 4 individuals transitioned from Motel Assistance to permanent housing
fields.key_decisions: Established a response process under the City of Reno and NDOT Interlocal Agreement (ILA) to address encampments on NDOT Right-of-Ways; Washoe County Guardian's Office agreed to take over motel payments for two clients who reached the limit of the Reno Housing Authority Motel Assistance Program; City of Reno and Cares Campus Collaboration meetings (launched Fall 2025) continued to streamline transitions of shelter clients with income into permanent housing
fields.action_items: Continue Mobile Camera Trailer Pilot Program at Spokane Street and Galletti Way locations; deliver Verification of Homelessness letter to individual at Wells Avenue & Kuenzli Street; submit Motel Assistance paperwork for client met at CARES Campus; identify and submit alternative housing applications for couple evicted from Cheryl Apartments and moved to EconoLodge; follow up on housing plan for client whose living arrangement with mother was ruled out at Competency Court hearing
